Container.Name: actual-sync
Container.Overview:
Automated bank transaction synchronization for Actual Budget. Runs scheduled syncs across one or many budgets (including encrypted budgets), with a web dashboard, Prometheus metrics, multi-channel notifications (Email/Telegram/Slack/Discord), retry logic, and health monitoring.
Two ways to configure: (1) for a SINGLE budget, just fill in the "Actual server URL / password / Sync ID" variables below - no config file needed. (2) For MULTIPLE budgets, leave those blank and put a config.json in the Config path (on first start a config.example.json template is seeded there). See the project README.
Container.Description: Automated bank transaction synchronization for Actual Budget with multi-server support, scheduling, encrypted-budget support, a web dashboard, Prometheus metrics, and multi-channel notifications. SINGLE budget (simplest): fill in the "Actual server URL", "Actual server password" and "Actual server Sync ID" variables below and start - no config file needed. (Sync ID is in Actual Budget → open the budget → Settings → Advanced → "Sync ID".) MULTIPLE budgets: leave those variables blank. On first start, if the Config directory has no config.json, the container writes a config.example.json template there and exits; fill in each server, rename it to config.json, and restart. config.json is validated at startup - an invalid one stops the container from starting (check the logs, or run 'npm run validate-config'), so fill the template correctly before renaming. If both a config.json and the variables are set, config.json wins (a budget is never synced twice). The health/dashboard/metrics endpoint is served on container port 3000 (/health, /ready, /metrics, /dashboard). Keep healthCheck.host as "0.0.0.0" in config.json. Do NOT set it to your server's LAN IP, or the dashboard will be unreachable in bridge mode.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: Directory containing config.json. config.json is validated against the project schema at startup: an INVALID config.json STOPS the container from starting (it will fail/restart in the logs), so fill in the seeded config.example.json correctly before renaming it to config.json. Wrong types, out-of-range values, and missing required fields are fatal; unknown or typo'd keys only warn. Check the container logs (or run 'npm run validate-config') if it will not start. Not needed for the single-budget env-var setup.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Persistent data: SQLite sync history and per-server Actual Budget data directories.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID the service runs as. On Unraid use 99 (nobody) so it can write the appdata directories.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID the service runs as. On Unraid use 100 (users).

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone used for scheduling and log timestamps, e.g. America/New_York.

Config.Name: Node Environment
Config.Description: Node.js runtime environment. Leave as production unless debugging.

Config.Name: Actual server URL
Config.Description: SINGLE-budget setup: your Actual Budget server URL, e.g. https://actual.example.com. Leave blank if you use a config.json for multiple budgets.

Config.Name: Actual server password
Config.Description: SINGLE-budget setup: your Actual Budget server password. Leave blank if you use a config.json.

Config.Name: Actual server Sync ID
Config.Description: SINGLE-budget setup: the budget Sync ID (Actual Budget → open the budget → Settings → Advanced → Sync ID). Leave blank if you use a config.json.

Config.Name: Actual server name
Config.Description: Optional display name for the single-budget setup (default: Default).

Config.Name: Actual server encryption password
Config.Description: Optional: end-to-end encryption password, only if the budget is E2EE-encrypted.

Config.Name: Actual server schedule
Config.Description: Optional cron schedule for the single-budget setup, e.g. 0 2 * * *. Defaults to the global schedule.

Config.Name: Actual server data dir
Config.Description: Optional: data directory for the single-budget setup (default: data/<name-slug>, which lands in the mounted Data volume). Keep it under data/ so it persists; a bare path like 'foo' resolves to /app/foo and is NOT in the Data volume. Leave blank unless you need a specific path.
